overview: This Mono-Black Midrange deck leverages efficient threats and versatile removal to control the board state while gradually outvaluing opponents in Standard. By combining early pressure with mid-game combat tricks, it aims to stabilize before transitioning into a late-game grind. The strategy relies on careful resource management and life total manipulation to secure victories.
win_conditions: Closing out games with unblockable or high-toughness threats
Draining opponent's life total with direct damage and combat tricks

keep_criteria: Hands with early land drops and one-mana threats
Hands containing Requiting Hex for early interaction
Balanced mana bases with at least two black sources
Keep hands with 2-3 lands, at least one turn-1 or turn-2 interaction (Requiting Hex, early removal), and a 2-drop threat or midrange engine.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Hands lacking early interaction or threats
Mulligan pure curve toppers, triple lands, or hands lacking early answers against spell-heavy matchups.

early_game: Deploy one-mana threats to apply pressure
Use Requiting Hex to clear blockers or answer early aggro threats
drop Requiting Hex or cheap removal if opponent plays a problematic early spell, otherwise develop mana.
Turn 2: Play a 2-drop threat or interaction depending on board state.
Prioritize lands that enter untapped or fetch sources if available
otherwise, sequence basics to ensure consistent black mana for threats and interaction.
mid_game: Maintain board presence with Unstoppable Slasher
Manage life totals and opponent's resources
Balance mana between interaction and threat development
avoid flooding by holding lands if you have sufficient answers.
Manage hand size to maintain card advantage, and use the graveyard strategically if the deck includes recursion or sacrifice outlets.
Commit threats when you have interaction to clear blockers or when the opponent is tapped out.
Hold back if facing sweepers, heavy counters, or if you need to preserve mana for protection.
Trade efficiently to maintain board presence without overextending.
late_game: Use excess card advantage to outlast opponents
Close out games with direct damage and combat pressure
Bloodletter of Aclazotz generating deathtouch tokens, Unstoppable Slasher with accumulated +1/+1 counters, or a well-timed Requiting Hex to protect a win condition or remove a key blocker.
Go all-in when the opponent has no interaction, is low on life, or you hold a decisive card advantage.
Commit when you can force through multiple threats or when your board state is resilient enough to absorb a single answer.

key_cards.name: Bloodletter of Aclazotz
key_cards.notes: Deploy on turn one to apply immediate pressure and force unfavorable trades for opponents.

key_cards.name: Unstoppable Slasher
key_cards.role: Mid-game board control and damage engine
key_cards.notes: Use its ability to remove blockers and deal direct damage, preserving your life total while advancing the board state.

key_cards.name: Requiting Hex
key_cards.notes: Cast to eliminate key threats or answer problematic permanents, often generating card advantage or life gain in the process.

sequencing_tips: Hold Requiting Hex for opponent's key threats rather than using it preemptively
Activate Unstoppable Slasher's ability after opponent commits to blockers
Prioritize land drops over early spells if mana base is tight
Avoid overcommitting to a single threat without backup.
Practice timing Requiting Hex to maximize its dual effect.
Watch for format-specific rules that may affect land sequencing or card legality.
Common mistake: holding too much mana for interaction and getting overrun
If the deck includes graveyard recursion, manage it carefully to avoid giving opponents free targets.

matchups.advice: Prioritize life total preservation and use Requiting Hex on their biggest threats. Deploy Bloodletter of Aclazotz early to force trades.
Apply steady pressure with threats and use combat tricks to bypass counterspells. Avoid overextending into sweepers.
Disrupt their combo pieces early with Requiting Hex. Keep life total high and use Unstoppable Slasher to clear their setup.

Use Requiting Hex to counter their counters or cast threats in response to their spells. Apply multiple threats to force through, and consider sacrificing or discarding to bait counters before committing your finisher.

Apply early pressure and use interaction to disrupt their mana development. Don't get outpaced; stabilize quickly with removal and transition to midrange threats before they can deploy their win conditions.

Win the resource war by sequencing better, sideboarding in targeted hate, and leveraging card advantage. Force them to use interaction inefficiently, and prioritize board presence over single threats.

common_threats: Watch for counterspells, sweepers, and combo win conditions.
Note when opponents tap out for big spells or leave mana open for interaction.
Track graveyard usage and life totals to time your finishers correctly.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Mono-Black Midrange archetype in Standard format

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Signature cards Bloodletter of Aclazotz and Unstoppable Slasher provide combat advantage and direct damage

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Standard midrange sequencing principles applied to signature card interactions

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Standard format meta trends and mono-black midrange role
